content.name: History & Civics Practice
content.description: General history and civics practice set for humanities testing and mixed-subject revision.

questions.question: Which civilisation is associated with cities such as Harappa and Mohenjo-daro?
questions.explanation: Harappa and Mohenjo-daro are key sites of the Indus Valley Civilisation.

questions.question: In a democracy, laws are generally made by:
questions.explanation: The legislature is the branch typically responsible for making laws.

questions.question: The French Revolution began in which year?
questions.explanation: The French Revolution is widely dated to 1789.

questions.question: Write a short answer explaining one reason why a written constitution is important in a democracy.
questions.model_answer: A written constitution defines the structure of government, protects rights, and sets limits on power so institutions remain accountable.
questions.explanation: A good answer should mention limits on state power, rights, rules, or accountability.

questions.question: Which institution usually interprets the constitution in a constitutional democracy?
questions.explanation: The judiciary, especially higher courts, interprets the constitution.
